evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my Kotlin Multiplatform app. Here's my shared screen model with no scanning wired up yet: EmptyScreenModel.kt. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app, shared between Android and iOS.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md and writes a complete BarcodeCapture integration into EmptyScreenModel.kt: DataCaptureContext.initialize with a license key placeholder, BarcodeCapture.forContext with BarcodeCaptureSettings.barcodeCaptureSettings() enabling EAN13_UPCA and CODE128, a Camera wired via setFrameSource, a BarcodeCaptureListener implementation, and a setupDataCaptureView function the platform hosts call. It also explains that Android and iOS each construct their own DataCaptureView (different constructor signatures) and embed it via toAndroidView()/toUIView().

assertions.text: com.kmp.datacapture.barcode.capture.BarcodeCapture is imported (not com.scandit.datacapture.*)
A license key placeholder string is present (e.g. -- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --)
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is used to create the context
BarcodeCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is used to configure the camera
Camera.getDefaultCamera( is used to obtain the camera
dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource( is called with the camera
BarcodeCaptureSettings.barcodeCaptureSettings() factory is used (not a bare BarcodeCaptureSettings() constructor)
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.CODE128 is enabled
No symbologies other than EAN13_UPCA and CODE128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture.forContext(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode (not forDataCaptureContext)
addListener( is called or equivalent listener registration
The screen model implements BarcodeCaptureListener with onBarcodeScanned and onSessionUpdated
onBarcodeScanned reads session.newlyRecognizedBarcode
A setupDataCaptureView (or equivalently named) function accepts a DataCaptureView and attaches a BarcodeCaptureOverlay via addOverlay
The response explains that Android's DataCaptureView constructor needs a Context while iOS's does not, so the view must be constructed on each platform host rather than in the shared screen model

evals.prompt: In my KMP BarcodeCapture screen model (IntegratedScreenModel.kt), when a barcode is scanned I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API with a suspend function, and re-enable scanning once the lookup completes.
evals.expected_output: The skill keeps barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false being set at the start of onBarcodeScanned (already present), launches a coroutine (e.g. via a CoroutineScope on the screen model) to perform the async lookup, and sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true once the lookup completes. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set inside onBarcodeScanned before the async lookup
A coroutine (launch or async on a CoroutineScope) is used to perform the async lookup
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true is set after the lookup completes
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, settings, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want silent scanning in my KMP BarcodeCapture app — suppress the default beep and vibration. Update IntegratedScreenModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill reassigns barcodeCapture.feedback to a BarcodeCaptureFeedback with no sound or vibration (the BarcodeCaptureFeedback() empty constructor, as opposed to BarcodeCaptureFeedback.defaultFeedback() which has sound/vibration). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.feedback is reassigned to suppress audio and haptic feedback (BarcodeCaptureFeedback() empty constructor or a Feedback with sound and vibration set to null, not BarcodeCaptureFeedback.defaultFeedback())
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, settings, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square rectangular viewfinder to my KMP BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim. Update IntegratedScreenModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs a RectangularViewfinder via RectangularViewfinder.withStyleAndLineStyle (or withStyle) with a SQUARE style, and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: RectangularViewfinder.withStyle( or RectangularViewfinder.withStyleAndLineStyle( is used with RectangularViewfinderStyle.SQUARE
The viewfinder is assigned to the overlay via overlay.viewfinder =
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, settings, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need an aimer-style crosshair viewfinder instead, for my KMP BarcodeCapture preview. Update IntegratedScreenModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs an AimerViewfinder() and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property. The skill does not suggest LaserlineViewfinder since it is not available on KMP. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: AimerViewfinder() is constructed with no arguments
The viewfinder is assigned to the overlay via overlay.viewfinder =
The deprecated-for-KMP LaserlineViewfinder is NOT suggested or used, since it does not exist in the KMP viewfinder package
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, settings, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My KMP BarcodeCapture screen model (IntegratedScreenModel.kt) doesn't clean up properly when the screen is left. Add a dispose function that removes the listener, removes the mode from the context, and turns off the camera.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a dispose() function that sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false, calls barcodeCapture.removeListener(this), calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(barcodeCapture), and calls camera?.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF), in that order. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.removeListener( is called in the new dispose function
dataCaptureContext.removeMode(barcodeCapture) is called (not removeCurrentMode or removeAllModes)
camera?.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.OFF) (or non-null equivalent) is called to turn off the camera
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set before removing the listener
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, settings, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored, in my KMP BarcodeCapture app (IntegratedScreenModel.kt).
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.codeDuplicateFilter = 500L (a plain Long, not a TimeInterval/TimeSpan wrapper type) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings before the mode is created, or applies it via barcodeCapture.applySettings(settings). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: codeDuplicateFilter is set to a Long value of 500 (500L or 500), not wrapped in TimeInterval or TimeSpan
codeDuplicateFilter is set on the BarcodeCaptureSettings before BarcodeCapture.forContext( or applied via barcodeCapture.applySettings(
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, settings, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to use Compose Multiplatform for my KMP BarcodeCapture screen instead of the imperative shared-ScreenModel pattern. Here's my empty Compose screen: EmptyComposeScreen.kt. Scan EAN-13 and QR codes.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the core-compose DataCaptureView composable: rememberDataCaptureContext(licenseKey) to create the context, rememberCamera(context) to wire and drive the camera, BarcodeCapture.forContext with BarcodeCaptureSettings.barcodeCaptureSettings() enabling EAN13_UPCA and QR, a remembered BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCapture(barcodeCapture), and passes overlays = listOf(overlay) into the DataCaptureView composable. It does not look for a BarcodeCaptureView composable (none exists) and flags that the overlay must be remember-ed to avoid recomposition churn.

assertions.text: rememberDataCaptureContext( is used to create the DataCaptureContext
rememberCamera( is used to wire the camera
BarcodeCapture.forContext( is used to create the mode
BarcodeCaptureSettings.barcodeCaptureSettings() is used
Symbology.EAN13_UPCA is enabled
Symbology.QR is enabled
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCapture( is used (not withBarcodeCaptureForView, since there is no imperative DataCaptureView to bind to)
The overlay instance is wrapped in remember( to keep it stable across recompositions
The DataCaptureView composable is called with overlays = listOf(overlay)
No BarcodeCaptureView composable is used or suggested, since BarcodeCapture has no dedicated Compose companion
